name: Seafood En Papillote
recipeIngredient: or other flat fish fillets, such as lemon sole
2 cups Fish stock (or clam juice, or chicken broth)
2 Green onions, sliced on diagonal (with green part saved)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Rinse flounder, shrimp, and scallops; set aside on a paper towel.
3. Melt butter in a skillet; stir in flour and cook until tan.
4. Gradually add fish stock while stirring constantly; bring to boil, reduce to simmer, and cook until reduced by half.
5. Add wine and season with salt and pepper.
6. Stir a little hot sauce into the egg yolks, then stir yolks back into the sauce.
7. Cook over low heat without overcooking yolks; strain if needed.
8. Prepare 4 individual pouches or one large one: cut parchment paper (or aluminum foil) into sheets and brush half with oil.
9. Place fillets in the center of a large sheet, or one fillet on each small sheet.
10. Distribute shrimp, scallops, mushrooms, green onion, and parsley over seafood.
11. Spoon sauce over seafood; top with reserved green onion and seal pouch tightly.
12. Bake until parchment browns, about 10 minutes.
13. Serve in pouches by slitting at the table, or transfer to a plate/ serving platter just before serving.
